title: Kung Pao Chicken Sichuan

seo.title: How to Make Kung Pao Chicken Sichuan — Easy Chinese Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make authentic Kung Pao Chicken Sichuan. This easy Chinese recipe includes step-by-step instructions, tips, and serving ideas.

seo.imageAlt: Authentic Chinese Kung Pao Chicken Sichuan recipe
seo.imageCaption: Delicious homemade Kung Pao Chicken Sichuan — a beloved Chinese classic

description: Kung Pao Chicken Sichuan is a fiery and flavorful stir-fry, a true classic from the Sichuan province of China. This iconic dish features tender chicken pieces, peanuts, and dried chilies tossed in a savory, sweet, and tangy sauce, delivering a delightful numbing and spicy kick known as mala. It is a perfect balance of textures and tastes, making it a beloved choice for those seeking an authentic Chinese culinary adventure.

ingredients.notes: cut into 1-inch pieces, seeds removed if desired
green parts only, cut into 1-inch pieces

instructions.title: Marinate the Chicken
instructions.text: In a medium bowl, combine the cubed chicken with 1 tablespoon light soy sauce, 1 tablespoon Shaoxing wine, and 1 tablespoon cornstarch. Mix well to coat the chicken evenly. Let it marinate for at least 15 minutes at room temperature, or up to 30 minutes in the refrigerator.

instructions.title: Prepare the Kung Pao Sauce
instructions.text: In a small bowl, whisk together the Chinkiang black vinegar, 1 tablespoon light soy sauce, 1 teaspoon dark soy sauce (if using), sugar, 1 teaspoon cornstarch, and water or chicken broth until smooth. Set aside. This is your Kung Pao sauce.

instructions.title: Stir-fry Aromatics and Chilies
instructions.text: Heat 2 tablespoons of peanut or vegetable oil in a wok or large skillet over high heat until smoking. Add the dried Sichuan chilies and whole Sichuan peppercorns. Stir-fry for 15-20 seconds until fragrant and the chilies are slightly darkened, being careful not to burn them. Quickly add the minced garlic and ginger and stir-fry for another 10-15 seconds.

instructions.title: Cook the Chicken
instructions.text: Push the aromatics to one side of the wok. Add the remaining 1 tablespoon of oil to the empty side, then add the marinated chicken in a single layer. Let the chicken sear undisturbed for 1-2 minutes until browned, then stir-fry until cooked through and lightly golden, about 3-4 minutes. Remove the chicken and aromatics from the wok and set aside.

instructions.title: Combine and Finish
instructions.text: Return the cooked chicken, chilies, and peppercorns to the wok. Add the chopped scallions and toasted peanuts. Give the prepared Kung Pao sauce a quick stir, then pour it over the ingredients in the wok. Stir-fry constantly for 1-2 minutes until the sauce thickens and coats all the ingredients evenly. The sauce should be glossy and bubbling.

instructions.title: Serve Immediately
instructions.text: Transfer the Kung Pao Chicken Sichuan immediately to a serving platter. Serve hot with steamed white rice for a complete and satisfying authentic Chinese meal. Enjoy the complex flavors and the signature mala sensation!

tips: For truly tender chicken, practice the velveting technique by adding a small amount of egg white to the marinade, ensuring the chicken pieces are coated and protected during cooking.
Do not skip the Sichuan peppercorns; they provide the characteristic numbing sensation (ma la) essential to authentic Sichuan cuisine. Toast them briefly before use to enhance their aroma.

Serve Kung Pao Chicken immediately with freshly steamed jasmine rice to best enjoy the contrasting textures and hot, spicy flavors. Leftovers can be stored in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 2-3 days.
article: Kung Pao Chicken Sichuan is a beloved Chinese Chicken Recipes dish enjoyed across Chinese. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
